text.title: Tool Use Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `tool-use-basics` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Every tool has a Pydantic input model + a typed return value. Untyped tools are rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Untyped tools lead to silent argument coercion bugs and make tool schema generation impossible.

rule.statement: The tool-use loop has a hard iteration cap (default 10). Exceeding the cap raises ToolLoopExhausted.
rule.rationale: Without a cap, an LLM stuck in a thought-loop runs until quota or wallet exhaustion.

rule.statement: Every tool invocation logs (tool_name, arguments_hash, latency_ms, success). Anonymous tool calls forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without audit logging, post-incident analysis cannot reconstruct what the agent did.

rule.statement: Tool names follow snake_case_action_object pattern (e.g., search_invoices, send_email). Vague names rejected.
rule.rationale: Vague tool names (`run`, `do_thing`) make the LLM hallucinate signatures and pick wrong tools.

rule.statement: Tool errors are returned as structured tool_result content, not raised. The LLM gets a chance to recover.
rule.rationale: Raising exceptions blows up the run; returning structured errors lets the LLM retry with corrected arguments.
